narrative:
=== CONGA IDEAL CUSTOMER PROFILE — FY2026 ===
Based on analysis of 14,277 closed-won and 23,482 closed-lost deals from FY2022–FY2026, supplemented by PROS competitive win/loss data and 1,878 East region prospect accounts. Overall win rate: 38%. Avg ACV (won): $13.8K.

TIER 1 — HIGHEST PRIORITY SEGMENTS:
- Financial Services, Banking & Insurance: 2,167 won deals, 41.8% win rate. Complex contracts, CLM and Composer high demand, large ACV.
- Services & Consulting: 2,645 won deals, 40.0% win rate. Highest deal volume, fast cycles, strong Composer adoption.
- Engineering & Construction: 653 won deals, 45.7% win rate. Document-heavy workflows drive Composer and CLM.
- Federal Government: 137 won deals, 55.7% win rate. Highest win rate of any segment. Compliance needs align well with CLM.

TIER 2 — STRONG FIT SEGMENTS:
- Healthcare & Life Sciences: 925 won deals, 35.7% win rate. Highest ACV of any vertical (>$50K deals lead all segments). Compliance drives CLM.
- Non-Profit: 749 won deals, 42.1% win rate. Often Salesforce-native. Composer very sticky.
- Education: 575 won deals, 40.9% win rate. Good win rate. Composer and CLM for vendor/grant contracts.
- Technology (HW/SW/Services): 1,641 won deals, 35.8% win rate. High volume. CPQ and CLM strong. Competitive market.

TIER 3 — DEPRIORITIZE:
- Manufacturing: 1,670 won deals, 33.2% win rate. Strong PROS competition.
- Retail & Restaurants: 466 won deals, 33.5% win rate. High churn risk, typically low ACV.
- Energy, Oil & Utilities: 391 won deals, 31.1% win rate. Long procurement cycles, limited Salesforce penetration.
- Transportation, Distribution & Logistics: 236 won deals, 29.0% win rate. Worst win rate of all segments. Avoid without a strong champion.

FIRMOGRAPHIC SWEET SPOT:
- Revenue: $100M–$5B. Companies <$50M rarely justify enterprise CLM/CPQ. >$5B often have custom builds.
- Employees: 500–5,000. Below 200: too small for ROI. Above 10K: longer cycles, more competitive.
- Geography: AMER primary (69% of all lost deals), EMEA secondary (22%). APAC growing opportunity.
- Salesforce: Active Salesforce CRM customer is the strongest single buy signal. Conga's core products live inside Salesforce.
- ERP: SAP, Oracle EBS, NetSuite. ERP = operational complexity = need for contract/revenue automation.
- Segment: Commercial (500–2,500 EEs) = highest volume. Enterprise = highest ACV.

PRODUCT-TO-SEGMENT FIT:
- CLM primary: Healthcare & Life Sciences, Financial Services & Banking, Technology, Federal & State Government
- CPQ primary: Manufacturing, Technology (strong secondary)
- Composer primary: Services & Consulting, Non-Profit & Education
- CLM secondary: Services & Consulting, Engineering & Construction, Non-Profit & Education

WHY DEALS ARE LOST:
- No Decision (48%): Lack of urgency, weak champion, stalled initiative. Fix: Executive sponsor early, tie to a business event (renewal, audit, M&A).
- Competitive Loss (13%): Competitor better positioned or longer relationship. Fix: Leverage ROI data, identify PROS/DocuSign displacement.
- Not Qualified (13%): Poor lead quality, wrong segment or size. Fix: Apply ICP filter before building pipeline.
- Duplicate/Admin (8%): Pipeline hygiene issue.
- Non-Competitive Loss (8%): Budget, timeline, internal reprioritization. Fix: Multi-thread, set next-step commitments at every stage.
KEY INSIGHT: Nearly half of all losses are No Decision. This is a qualification and urgency problem, not a product problem.

BUYING SIGNALS & TECH STACK:
Positive signals: Active Salesforce CRM user (strongest), SAP or Oracle ERP in place, currently using DocuSign or Adobe Sign (displacement opportunity), manual document generation via Word/Excel, legacy contract repository (shared drive, SharePoint).
Business event triggers: Recent M&A activity, compliance or regulatory audit in past 12 months, new CFO or CLO, Salesforce platform expansion or upgrade, headcount reduction driving automation urgency.

COMPETITIVE CONTEXT — PROS:
PROS customers run: Salesforce CRM (57%), Microsoft Dynamics (22%), SAP CRM (8%). ERP: SAP all variants (55%), MS Dynamics 365 (7%), Oracle EBS (7%).
Displacement signal: PROS-using accounts in Manufacturing and Industrial segments that also run Salesforce are ideal displacement targets for Conga CPQ. Lead with total cost of ownership and Salesforce-native integration advantages.

WHO NOT TO TARGET:
- Companies under 200 employees or below $50M revenue.
- No Salesforce CRM (requires significant integration work, lacks native value prop).
- Transportation/Logistics (29%), Energy/Oil/Utilities (31%), Retail/Restaurants (33%) — persistent underperformance.
- No executive sponsor (48% of losses are No Decision — without exec champion, deals stall).
- Startups and early-stage companies (lack contract volume, procurement maturity, budget).

QUICK REFERENCE — IDEAL ACCOUNT:
✅ Industry: Financial Services, Services & Consulting, Engineering & Construction, Federal Govt, or Healthcare
✅ Revenue: $100M–$5B
✅ Employees: 500–5,000
✅ Tech: Salesforce CRM + SAP or Oracle ERP
✅ Trigger: M&A, compliance audit, Salesforce expansion, new CLO/CFO
✅ Buyer: CLO, VP Legal, VP Sales Ops, VP Finance
✅ Product: CLM (high ACV) or Composer (high volume)

PASS OR PARK IF:
❌ Industry: Transportation/Logistics, Energy/Oil/Utilities, Retail/Restaurants
❌ Under 200 employees or below $50M revenue
❌ Not on Salesforce CRM
❌ No identified executive champion
❌ No clear business trigger or event
❌ Early-stage or pre-revenue company
❌ Budget not yet allocated or initiative deprioritized
